title: Algorithmic Credit Bias
description: I want to understand how credit‑scoring algorithms may discriminate against minority groups.
narrative: The user seeks research on international studies, legal analyses, and empirical data that demonstrate statistical bias in credit‑scoring models. Relevant documents should discuss methodology, impact on specific demographic groups, and mitigation strategies; irrelevant sources that only mention credit cards, gift cards, or general financial advice should be excluded.

title: Urban Zoning Economics
description: How do recent sustainable urban zoning bylaws affect property values and economic activity in city centers?
narrative: The user is a city planner looking for evidence-based analysis on the economic impact of new sustainability-focused zoning rules. They need studies, data, or expert commentary that link policy changes to measurable changes in property values, commercial activity, and investment patterns over time.

title: Urban Microgreens
description: What are the best practices for cultivating microgreens in urban apartment settings?
narrative: The user is a city dweller who wants to grow microgreens in limited space, seeking practical guidance on indoor growth systems, nutrient use, and sanitation. Relevant documents should cover space‑efficient methods, cost analysis, and safety standards.

title: AI in education ethics
description: What ethical guidelines should students and academic institutions follow when implementing AI tools in educational settings?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of ethical frameworks, risk assessments, and best practices for AI in education, including legal considerations and real‑world case studies, while excluding general marketing or promotional material.

title: Urban Transit Sustainability
description: What are the most effective policies to promote sustainable urban transportation in mid‑sized cities?
narrative: The user is a city planner looking for evidence‑based policy recommendations that balance environmental, economic, and social impacts. Documents should include studies on specific policy interventions (e.g., congestion pricing, bike‑share programs, transit subsidies), cost‑benefit analyses, case studies of mid‑sized cities, and reports on modal shift outcomes. Exclude general academic reviews, unrelated transportation metrics, or policy proposals aimed solely at large metropolitan areas.

title: Pandemic AI Outcomes
description: What measurable impacts did AI-driven contact tracing have on COVID-19 case reductions in early 2020 across different countries?
narrative: The user seeks quantitative, peer-reviewed evidence on the effectiveness of AI-enabled contact tracing during the initial wave of COVID-19, comparing case reduction metrics, app adoption rates, and policy environments, with an emphasis on studies published between 2020 and 2022 that provide detailed statistical analyses and cross-national comparisons.

title: Campus e‑Mobility
description: Which electric bike or scooter is more suitable for university students commuting on campus, considering cost, range, and regulations?
narrative: The user is a student who needs an affordable, lightweight mobility solution to travel between classes, dorms, and campus facilities. They are evaluating e‑bikes versus e‑scooters, focusing on purchase or lease costs, battery capacity and range for typical campus distances, charging infrastructure availability on campus, comfort and weight for carrying class materials, maintenance requirements, and local university or city bylaws that govern e‑scooter use on campus walkways and bike lanes. Relevance requires sources that provide empirical data, comparative studies, or specific case reports from universities that have implemented student e‑mobility programs.

title: Energy Policy Ukraine
description: What has been the impact of the Ukraine war on European energy policy and what specific measures have been implemented to mitigate these effects?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst interested in how the Russian invasion of Ukraine has altered energy security strategies across the EU, especially in terms of investment initiatives, diversification efforts, and regulatory changes that were adopted in response to the crisis. Relevant documents should discuss energy supply disruptions, new EU funding programs, changes to import sources, legislation, and timelines directly tied to the war. Documents that merely report on the war itself, unrelated economic topics, or unrelated regional events should be considered irrelevant.

title: AI Privacy Compliance
description: How can an AI‑healthcare startup ensure compliance with GDPR and HIPAA when processing patient data?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed, up‑to‑date guidance on regulatory requirements, data‑handling best practices, risk assessment templates, and real‑world case studies for AI applications in healthcare. Any document that offers actionable compliance steps, references to specific GDPR articles and HIPAA rules, and illustrative implementation examples is considered relevant.

title: Retirement Investment Planning
description: What diversified investment strategies can retirees use to balance growth and income while managing risk?
narrative: The user is a retiree seeking portfolio advice that addresses longevity, income generation, risk tolerance, and sector allocation. Relevant documents should discuss asset allocation frameworks, expected returns, risk metrics, and strategies that adjust for lifespan and market volatility.

title: Sustainable Packaging Materials
description: Which biodegradable materials are most effective and economically feasible for packaging in the food industry?
narrative: The user seeks comparative evidence on material properties, life‑cycle environmental impact, cost‑effectiveness, supply chain availability, regulatory compliance, and consumer acceptance for biodegradable packaging options suitable for food products.

title: Potato Disease Forecast
description: Which machine learning models best predict late blight outbreaks in European potato fields?
narrative: The user is a researcher looking for peer‑reviewed studies that develop, evaluate, and compare ML models for predicting late blight in European potato cultivation; relevant documents must include methodology, data sources, validation metrics, and case study results, and should not be general product information or historical overviews.

title: Adolescent Mental Health
description: What comprehensive evidence exists regarding the effectiveness of school‑based mental health interventions for adolescents aged 12‑18?
narrative: The user seeks recent systematic reviews and meta‑analyses (2015‑2023) that evaluate school‑based mental health programs aimed at reducing depression, anxiety, and suicide ideation among adolescents. Relevance requires peer‑reviewed journal articles or official policy reports that detail the intervention design, methodology, outcome measures, and effect sizes. Documents that are blog posts, conference announcements, or unrelated event listings are to be considered irrelevant.

title: Postwar Labor Dynamics
description: What impact did France’s labor history from 1945‑1980 have on contemporary HR practices, especially in digital and remote work environments?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly analyses or primary sources that trace key labor movements, reforms, and collective bargaining agreements in postwar France and connect them to modern organizational practices in technology companies. Relevance is judged by the presence of historical detail (e.g., dates, statutes, strikes), explicit discussion of HR or management implications, and critical assessment of how these historical forces shape current remote or digital work cultures.

title: Remote‑Learning Mental Health Impact
description: How did the shift to remote learning during 2021 affect the mental health of students in low‑income U.S. school districts?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed articles, policy reports, and case studies that analyze changes in anxiety, depression, and behavioral outcomes among K‑12 students in low‑income districts during the 2021 remote‑learning period. Relevance requires the source to focus specifically on U.S. low‑income school districts, include empirical data collected between January 2021 and December 2021, and discuss mental health indicators. Non‑U.S. studies, general education technology reviews, or surveys outside the 2021 window are excluded.

title: Digital Heritage Tourism
description: What digital tools and best‑practice strategies enable visitors to discover and experience historical sites while preserving their sense of place?
narrative: The user seeks guidance on technologies such as augmented reality, mobile apps, and interactive maps that enhance heritage tourism. They want to understand how these tools can improve visitor engagement, accessibility, and authenticity without compromising the on‑site experience. Relevant documents should discuss case studies, user‑experience design, technology integration, evaluation metrics, and cultural‑heritage preservation; general tourist guides or unrelated historical texts are not pertinent.

title: Urban Microclimates
description: How do green roofs influence urban temperature and air quality in megacities?
narrative: I am researching interventions to mitigate urban heat islands and pollution; I need studies that (1) quantify temperature reduction and VOC/NOx changes on buildings with green roofs, (2) focus on cities with populations over one million, and (3) include real‑world monitoring data rather than purely theoretical models.

title: Responsible AI Principles
description: What are the main principles guiding responsible AI development and deployment?
narrative: The user seeks a concise synthesis of ethical frameworks, legal requirements, and industry best practices that shape responsible AI. Relevance criteria include coverage of data privacy, fairness, transparency, accountability, and societal impact, with emphasis on technical guidance for implementation. Exclude purely theoretical discourse, outdated regulations, or vendor‑specific marketing material.

title: Hotel options near Espace Charenton
description: Which hotels near Espace Charenton offer good value, free Wi‑Fi, and easy access to public transport?
narrative: The user is planning a brief stay in Paris and wants to know which hotels close to Espace Charenton meet their standards: comfortable rooms, a solid rating, affordable rates, free Wi‑Fi, and proximity to metro lines. Relevance requires hotel listings with price, facilities, and transport details; unrelated store information, general hotel reviews, or apartment options should be excluded.

title: EU Renewable Subsidies
description: How have recent EU directives altered subsidies for renewable energy projects in member states?
narrative: The user seeks detailed analysis of policy changes, their impact on funding levels, eligibility criteria, and resulting cost implications for renewable energy projects across EU countries. They need up-to-date, comparative data and authoritative sources.

title: Leasing vs. Buying EVs
description: Should I lease or buy an electric vehicle, and what are the long-term financial and environmental implications of each choice?
narrative: The user wants to evaluate the pros and cons of leasing versus buying an electric car, focusing on factors such as upfront costs, monthly payments, lease terms, mileage limits, battery degradation, maintenance responsibilities, residual value, resale value, tax incentives, and long-term ownership savings. Relevant documents should include comparative cost analyses, studies on battery wear and replacement costs, examples of lease agreements, consumer reports on total cost of ownership, and environmental impact assessments of both ownership models. Documents that discuss only generic car financing or that neglect the specific aspects of electric vehicle batteries and incentive structures should be excluded. The relevance criteria emphasize comprehensive, data‑driven comparisons that address the nuanced trade‑offs a prospective EV customer must consider.

title: Comic‑to‑Film Adaptations
description: What are the key milestones and challenges in turning the Asterix comics into live‑action and animated movies?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of the adaptation history of the Asterix comic series, including dates, director and writer involvement, production hurdles, stylistic choices, fidelity to the source, critical reception, box‑office performance, and the impact on the franchise’s legacy. All cited sources must detail these aspects and differentiate between animated and live‑action editions.

title: Carbon‑neutral trip to Faro
description: How can I plan a carbon‑neutral travel itinerary to Faro, Portugal, including sustainable flight options, low‑impact accommodation, and eco‑friendly ground transport?
narrative: The user wants to minimize carbon emissions for a trip to Faro. They are looking for information on flight carbon‑offset programs, hotels with green certifications, and transportation alternatives that reduce environmental impact (public transit, bike rentals, walking tours). Relevant documents should provide or compare sustainable flight choices, eco‑friendly lodging in Faro, and local low‑emission ground‑transport options. Documents that merely list flight prices or general car‑rental information without addressing sustainability are considered irrelevant.

title: Startup Funding Trends
description: What are the recent investment trends for early‑stage technology startups in Europe over the past three years?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date quantitative and qualitative data on venture capital activity, focusing on seed and Series A rounds, geographical concentration, and sector performance. Relevant sources should provide aggregated funding data and analyses from VC firms or market research rather than purely anecdotal case studies.

title: Teen Mental Health
description: What is the effect of social media usage on the mental well‑being of adolescents?
narrative: I am looking for contemporary research, systematic reviews, and empirical studies that examine the relationship between social media habits and psychological outcomes among teenagers, including anxiety, depression, self‑esteem, and sleep patterns. Relevance requires evidence‑based sources, peer‑reviewed journals, and recent data (last 10 years).

title: Beaujolais Health Concerns
description: What health risks are associated with Beaujolais Nouveau consumption, and what safe consumption guidelines exist?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on the health implications of consuming Beaujolais Nouveau wine. Relevant sources should discuss alcohol-related health risks, specific compounds in the wine that may affect health, empirical studies or reviews, and public health or medical recommendations for safe consumption. Content that is purely promotional, marketing, or unrelated to health considerations is not relevant.

title: Bias Detection in NLP Models
description: What are best practices for detecting and mitigating bias in large language models?
narrative: The user seeks detailed methods, evaluation metrics, tools, and real‑world case studies for identifying and reducing bias in NLP systems, with an emphasis on practical audits and unsupervised techniques.

title: EV Adoption Factors
description: What are the primary factors influencing urban commuters' adoption of electric vehicles, and how do city-level policies affect this decision?
narrative: The user seeks to understand the key drivers and barriers for EV adoption among city drivers, focusing on economic incentives, charging infrastructure, and regulatory measures. Relevant documents should contain quantitative studies, policy reports, and urban transport analyses that assess these variables. Documents that merely list vehicle specifications or unrelated brand promotions are considered irrelevant.

title: AI in Education
description: How can AI be used to personalize learning in secondary schools?
narrative: The user wants evidence‑based insights on integrating AI tools into high‑school instruction to tailor content, assess progress, and increase engagement. Relevant documents report pilot studies, outcome analyses, or meta‑reviews on AI‑driven adaptive learning systems, while irrelevant marketing or non‑educational AI narratives are excluded.

title: Mountain Touring
description: What car rental options best suit a trip to Reunion's mountainous interior, including Piton de la Fournaise and Mafate, with attention to fuel type, insurance, GPS, and age restrictions?
narrative: The user seeks a vehicle suitable for steep terrain, fuel‑efficient, equipped with GPS, low deductible insurance, compatible with under‑25 drivers, and capable of carrying a mixed‑age group. Relevant results should detail vehicle specs, insurance policies, GPS availability, and age‑based fees.

title: Battery Degradation in Used EVs
description: What is the typical battery capacity loss after 100,000 km in used electric vehicles?
narrative: The user is a prospective used EV buyer who wants to evaluate how much battery degradation to expect at around 100,000 km mileage. Relevance is determined by documents that provide estimates of battery health, degradation rates, warranties, and third‑party studies relevant to used EVs. Mentions of vehicle brand, model, year, mileage, and battery chemistry are expected.

title: Cannabis Driving Law
description: What are the legal penalties, testing procedures, and enforcement details for driving under the influence of cannabis in France, especially when combined with alcohol?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative, up‑to‑date information on French law regarding driving after cannabis consumption. Relevant documents should describe statutory penalties, blood/urine test thresholds, procedures for immediate licensing actions, and the impact of combined alcohol use. Documents that merely mention cannabis in passing or discuss it in other contexts (e.g., medical use) are not relevant.

title: NLP Annotation Metrics
description: What are the standard metrics used to evaluate the quality of crowdsourced annotations in NLP datasets?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive understanding of evaluation techniques for crowdsourced NLP annotation. Relevant documents should discuss quantitative measures (e.g., inter‑annotator agreement, Cohen’s kappa, Krippendorff’s alpha, precision/recall for labeled data) as well as qualitative guidelines for acceptable thresholds, validation workflows, or tooling that implements these metrics. Content unrelated to annotation quality assessment or that focuses solely on data collection logistics without metric discussion should be considered irrelevant.

title: Mindfulness Anxiety Adolescents
description: I want to find peer‑reviewed studies that examine the long‑term effectiveness of mindfulness‑based interventions for reducing anxiety symptoms in school‑aged adolescents, including methodological details and outcomes in academic and social settings.
narrative: The user is a clinical psychologist researching evidence‑based treatments for adolescent anxiety. They need comprehensive scientific articles that specifically evaluate mindfulness programs in secondary schools, focusing on sustained outcomes (at least 12 months), control groups, effect sizes, and reported implementation challenges. Relevant documents should provide detailed methodology and quantitative results.

title: Preschool Bilingual Outcomes
description: What studies assess the academic and social outcomes of bilingual education for preschool‑aged children?
narrative: The user is looking for peer‑reviewed research that compares bilingual preschool programs to monolingual ones, focusing on cognitive development, language acquisition, and socio‑emotional skills, and wants recent studies (within the last ten years) with appropriate preschool populations.

title: Crypto Energy
description: How does the electricity consumption of cryptocurrency mining compare to other global industries, and what are the environmental impacts and policy responses?
narrative: The user seeks quantitative, up‑to‑date comparisons of crypto‑mining energy use against sectors such as transportation, manufacturing, and telecommunications, including per‑coin metrics, carbon‑footprint calculations, renewable‑energy penetration, and regulatory measures that could mitigate environmental effects. Relevant sources are peer‑reviewed studies, official reports, or comprehensive market analyses; marketing blogs or isolated anecdotes are not relevant.

title: Sustainable Photographic Materials
description: What are the environmental impacts of modern photographic chemicals and what sustainable alternatives exist for film development?
narrative: The user is a photographer researching eco‑friendly practices and seeks recent studies, data on chemical toxicity, lifecycle analyses, and industry initiatives that compare conventional and sustainable materials for film development.

title: LiDAR Mapping Approaches
description: What are the most reliable techniques for reconstructing urban terrain using LiDAR data, and how do they compare in accuracy and computational cost?
narrative: The user is a GIS analyst seeking comparative analysis of LiDAR mapping methods for urban environments. Relevance should include academic papers, industry reports, or case studies that describe algorithms, evaluation metrics, and practical trade‑offs.

title: Medieval Manuscripts Budget
description: I am looking for used books on medieval German manuscript illumination that are priced under ten euros.
narrative: The user seeks authoritative texts on a specialized subject with a strict budget; relevant results must be used copies, specifically about medieval German manuscript illumination and priced under €10.

title: AI‑Assisted Diagnosis
description: How can artificial intelligence improve the accuracy and efficiency of diagnosing rare diseases in primary care settings?
narrative: The user wants to evaluate the benefits, challenges, and implementation strategies of AI tools for rare disease diagnosis, including evidence from clinical studies, cost‑benefit analyses, and guidelines on integration with existing workflows. Relevance requires information that discusses AI diagnostic systems, rare diseases, primary care, and practical deployment considerations.

title: SIM Unlock Guide
description: How can I recover my SIM card’s PUK code and safely unlock my phone after too many incorrect attempts?
narrative: The user has blocked their mobile by entering the wrong PUK code repeatedly and now needs reliable information on how to retrieve the correct code from official SFR channels. They are looking for step‑by‑step instructions, phone numbers, online procedures, and precautions to avoid permanent lockout or data loss. Relevant content includes official support contacts, legal methods to obtain the code, and troubleshooting tips; irrelevant ads, unrelated service plans, or promotional material should be excluded.

title: Lamp History Impact
description: What cultural and technological impacts did early oil lamps have on 19th‑century European society?
narrative: The user seeks a scholarly examination of how oil lamps influenced urban life, public safety, and industrial productivity during the 1800s. Relevant documents should include contemporaneous engineering reports, urban planning records, and social commentaries from 1800‑1890, and should contrast different lamp types and their technical features in the context of societal change.

title: Biotech Regulatory Impact
description: How have recent FDA and EMA regulatory changes affected the funding landscape, operational costs, and strategic decisions of small biotechnology startups?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview that links specific policy updates to tangible business outcomes for emerging biotech firms, including case studies, financial metrics, and timeline analyses to assess the real-world impact of regulatory shifts.

title: Balancing Yield & Soil Health
description: How can farmers incorporate perennial cover crops to maintain short‑term production while improving soil health over several decades?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based analysis—including yield statistics, economic modeling, carbon sequestration estimates, and policy incentive reviews—that illustrates the trade‑off between immediate crop output and long‑term soil health benefits. Relevance requires documents that provide quantitative results, comparative studies, or rigorous case‑study data, rather than purely theoretical or anecdotal discussions.

title: Digital Literacy
description: What are the most effective strategies for enhancing digital literacy among elderly residents in metropolitan cities?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive review of instructional designs, technology tools, engagement models, cultural considerations, and measurable impacts; relevant materials should include peer‑reviewed studies, best‑practice guidelines, and case reports that evaluate skill gains, user satisfaction, and long‑term adoption.
